Semaglutide is a GLP-1 receptor agonist indicated for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus, prevention and treatment of obesity, and related metabolic disorders including cardiovascular risk reduction.

A key embodiment involves administering a GLP-1 agonist with a minimum half-life of 72 hours in doses of at least 0.7 mg weekly (or semaglutide-equivalent) on a once-weekly or less frequent schedule for managing type 2 diabetes, hyperglycemia, glucose tolerance disorders, and obesity.

semaglutide became the first long-acting GLP-1 receptor agonist to be approved by the United States Food and Drug Administration for management of obesity.

Tirzepatide is a dual GIP/GLP-1 receptor agonist developed by Eli Lilly for the treatment of type 2 diabetes mellitus and obesity.
In this 72-week trial in participants with obesity, 5 mg, 10 mg, or 15 mg of tirzepatide once weekly provided substantial and sustained reductions in body weight.
